name: Ipci et al. Synthetic Response Quality Evaluation Extract
population_or_context: Open-ended logical-proof responses in a discrete mathematics course.
sample_size: 585 authentic open-ended logical-proof responses are reported as the real-response comparator.
methods: Extracted the reported quality-evaluation approach for knowledge-profile synthetic responses, including diversity, realism, embedding/PCA comparison, and training-utility evidence.
education_context: Higher-education discrete mathematics logical-proof responses with analytic rubric alignment.
response_origin: LLM-generated synthetic responses are evaluated against authentic student responses from the same response setting.
label_source: Knowledge profiles and representative examples guide synthetic generation; authentic responses and rubric labels provide the comparison boundary.
generation_model: Large language models; model details are not fully specified in the repository metadata page.
generation_method: Profile-based LLM generation guided by knowledge profiles and representative examples.
prompt_strategy: Straightforward profile use and error-informed profile use are reported as profile-based strategies.
evaluation_method: Quality is evaluated through diversity and realism analysis, embedding-based distance metrics and PCA comparison, and downstream utility for automated grading model training.
scorer_use: Synthetic responses are considered for training augmentation and formative-feedback model development, not as standalone scorer-validation evidence.
real_response_comparator: Authentic logical-proof responses from the same educational context are the comparator.
reported_metrics: Repository metadata reports N = 585 authentic responses and summarizes diversity, realism, and model-training utility comparisons.
limitations: The public repository page summarizes the method and results but does not expose every prompt or model detail.
The evidence is bounded to discrete mathematics logical-proof responses.
Synthetic-only training is not established as a replacement for real response data.
